questions.description: Tom owns a house in the city centre of Dublin which he purchased in 1991. Advise Tom.
questions.text: Tom owns a house in the city centre of Dublin which he purchased in 1991. Just beyond the boundary of his garden is a small patch of waste land, which is owned by Dublin City Council. Tom has been parking his car on this waste land since he purchased his house. From about 1995, he began cultivating this place of waste land as a garden and around this time he constructed some temporary sheds on the land. In 2006, concerned about anti-social behaviour of some youths who had taken to loitering on the strip of land, he constructed a wall around it. In 2015, the Council wrote to Tom offering to lease the land to him at a low rent on condition that it is only used as a garden. Tom replied by letter stating simply that he would consider the proposal. There had been no further correspondence until recently, with the Council threatening to evict Tom from the strip of land as he is trespassing, and the land is needed as part of a development project by the Council to widen a nearby road. Advise Tom.

Answer (a) [OR]{.underline} (b). Do not answer both. (a) Discuss the manner in which the Land and Conveyancing Law Reform Act 2009 reforms the law relating to freehold covenants. [OR]{.underline} (b) Outline and discuss the various means by which one can acquire an easement, aside from express grant or reservation.

questions.description: Answer (a) [OR]{.underline} (b). Advise Joan and Aoife to whether they can keep the found goods, and if some other…
questions.text: Answer (a) [OR]{.underline} (b). Do not answer both. (a) Joan and Aoife are two students living in Kildare. One evening, Joan watches a documentary on the subject of valuable historical artefacts, and the income that can be made by selling such items at auction. Motivated by this financial gain, Joan purchases two metal detectors and convinces Aoife to join her in searching for valuable historical artefacts on a nearby farm. The search is a success, and they locate a very ancient necklace (c.800ad) and some wooden ancient culinary items. Joan and Aoife dig up the items and take them home. On their way home, Joan and Aoife find a brand new smart phone on the ground. Advise Joan and Aoife to whether they can keep the found goods, and if some other party or parties might have a better claim to them. [OR]{.underline} (b) Outline and discuss the law relating to contractual licences. Your answer should consider whether such licences constitute an interest in land and the impact that the procedural fusion of law and equity by the Judicature Acts had upon the type of remedies available to breech of the terms of the licence.

Answer (a) [OR]{.underline} (b). Do not answer both. (a) Outline and discuss the manner in which property devolves in the event of intestacy. Your answer should indicate the respective priority of all of the possible next-of-kin. [OR]{.underline} (b) Discuss the scope and extent of duty of a testator, as set out in section 117 of the Succession Act 1965, to make 'proper provision' for his children in accordance with his means.

questions.description: Outline and discuss [ONE]{.underline} of the following: (i) Discuss the strengthened protections that the Land and…
questions.text: Outline and discuss [ONE]{.underline} of the following: (i) Discuss the strengthened protections that the Land and Conveyancing Law Reform Acts 2009-2013 afford to mortgagors (i.e borrowers who have granted a mortgage to a lender as security for a loan) who have defaulted on their loand. [OR]{.underline} (ii) Analyse the extent to which the family home is afforded special protection under Irish law setting out the nature and extent of any such special legal protections. [OR]{.underline} (iii) Outline and discuss how one may acquire security of tenure in rented residential accommodation pursuant to the Residential Tenancies Act 2004, as amended, (sometimes referred to as a "part 4 tenancy"), as well as discussing the requirements that the Act sets out for a landlord who wishes to validly terminate a tenancy governed by the Act.

questions.description: Mary holds the lease on a small unit in Dublin City Centre which she runs… Advise Mary on any legal remadies that may…
questions.text: Mary holds the lease on a small unit in Dublin City Centre which she runs as a newsagents. The lease only contains clauses indicating the term ( of four years) of the lease, together with a covenant for the payment of rent. Due to the recession, Mary's business has not been performing as well as expected and she has missed a number of rent payments. On the 1st March she was served with a notice by the landlord requiring her to clear the arrears of rent within 24 hours, and this notice stated that a failure to do so would result in her forfeiting the lease. Two days after the notice was served the landlord and his agents broke the locks of the unit and entered the premises overnight. They clumsily removed all of the stock, together with the fixtures and fittings of the newsagent, and dumped it in storage for her to collect. She is now excluded from her business, and comes to you seeking urgent advice as to any remedy she might have against the landlord. Advise Mary on any legal remadies that may be available to her.

questions.description: Outline and discuss the distinction between co-owned property that is held pursuant to a joint tenancy and co-owned…
questions.text: Outline and discuss the distinction between co-owned property that is held pursuant to a joint tenancy and co-owned property that is held pursuant to a tenancy in common, setting out also why this distinction is important.

Answer (a) [AND]{.underline} (b). (a) Concisely set down the legal requirements that must be followed to execute a valid will, as well as any other technical matters that must be borne in mind when drafting such a will so as to ensure the testator's intentions are given full affect. [AND]{.underline} (b) Outline and assess the Irish Courts' approach to the admissibility of extrinsic evidence in the construction of wills.

questions.description: Critically evaluate the legislative protection afforded spouses in relation to family property in Ireland.
questions.text: Critically evaluate the legislative protection afforded spouses in relation to family property in Ireland. Support your answer with reference to appropriate authority.

questions.description: Simon has issued a new lease to Roger of a premises which lies in the… Company.
questions.text: Simon has issued a new lease to Roger of a premises which lies in the basement of his building. The basement lies below and adjacent to property retained by Simon. The lease agreement specifically states that the basement premises will be used for an Indian restaurant. However, upon inspection of the new establishment, the local Health Authority has stated that the sewerage system which is in place is wholly inadequate and all the piping will have to be replaced. Roger has acted accordingly and has hired a company to install the new piping which runs all the way through the property that is retained by the landlord. Simon immediately questions Roger's right to do this and now seeks your advice. Advise Simon. Please refer to relevant case law in your answer.

questions.description: Should the contractual licence be regarded as an interest in land in Irish Law?
questions.text: Should the contractual licence be regarded as an interest in land in Irish Law? Discuss your answer with reference to applicable case law.

questions.description: Jackie died suddenly from a short illness in January 2019. Advise Matthew and Mary.
questions.text: Jackie died suddenly from a short illness in January 2019. At the time of her death, she was living on the family farm (which she had inherited some 20 years previous) with her wife Julia. In her will, Jackie has left all of her worldly belongings to her son, Matthew. Jackie also has a daughter, Mary, from a previous relationship to whom she left nothing under the will. Upon discovering her mother's death, Mary claims she was left out of the will as she did not get along with her mother. However, she does acknowledge that her mother gave her €15,000 10 years ago to start up a new business venture which never took off. In fact, Mary was challenged by alcohol and drug addiction at the time and mishandled the money as a result. Mary has been in recovery for the past five years and is currently unemployed and living in local authority housing. Matthew, on the other hand, is married with three children and is a wealthy dentist. Advise Matthew and Mary.

questions.description: According to Wylie, '[o]ne of the most striking features of our land law has been the extent to which it has been…
questions.text: According to Wylie, '[o]ne of the most striking features of our land law has been the extent to which it has been influenced by principles of equity'. - Irish Land Law (5th ed.) 2013, (Dublin/ UK: Bloomsbury) p.91. Critically discuss the above statement, supporting your answer with relevant legal authority.

questions.description: In 2004, Ann decided to start growing some organic vegetables in a field behind her house.
questions.text: In 2004, Ann decided to start growing some organic vegetables in a field behind her house. After she had them all planted, she had a small shed erected which she used as a storage space for her gardening tools. A couple of months later, Ann planted a large hedge along the boundary to act as a windbreak. Barry, the registered owner of the field, lived in Spain. While holidaying in Ireland in 2016, Barry drove past the field only to see all the work that Ann had done on it. Ann happened to be out pulling up some weeds at the time. Barry told Ann that he had no present use for the field so she could continue her great work but he did intend to seek planning permission to build a house on it in the future. In the meantime, Barry told Ann that he would be happy to grant her a short lease at a nominal fee. Ann asked him to write to her solicitor about this but despite the fact that negotiations followed, no lease was ever granted. Ann died in 2018, leaving all her property to her daughter, Sheila, who resided with her. Barry died in 2014, leaving all his property to his son Dermot. Dermot is in poor health and lives in a nursing home. Sheila seeks your advice on whether or not she can be registered as owner of the field.

questions.description: In order for a will to be valid, there are a number of capacity and formality requirements which must be adhered to in…
questions.text: In order for a will to be valid, there are a number of capacity and formality requirements which must be adhered to in accordance with the Succession Act 1965. Critically discuss these requirements supporting your answer with sufficient and relevant case law.

questions.description: Following a night out with some friends in the local village, David… Advise David, citing relevant legal authority in…
questions.text: Following a night out with some friends in the local village, David decided to take a short cut home through a field which was on the way to his house. While trespassing on this property, he tripped over something which was half buried in the soil. Upon further investigation, he realised that he had discovered what appeared to be a very valuable silver chalice. Margaret, who had taken out a ninety-nine-year lease of the field the previous year, claimed that she should be entitled to the find. As word got around the local village, Margaret's landlord, Tim, heard about the find and claimed that since he owned the field, he should be entitled to the chalice. However, David now claims that since he was the one to discover the chalice, he should be entitled to keep it. Advise David, citing relevant legal authority in your answer.
